title: True or False? — Malala biography
subtitle: 10 statements about Malala Yousafzai. Decide based on the biography (Track A-B)

items.text: Malala Yousafzai was born in Pakistan in 1997.
items.explanation: True. Malala was born in Mingora, Pakistan, in July 1997. Her father was a teacher who valued education.

items.text: Malala won the Nobel Peace Prize at age 25.
items.explanation: Wrong. She won the Nobel Peace Prize in 2014 at age 17 — making her the youngest Nobel laureate ever.

items.text: Malala was attacked by the Taliban for going to school.
items.explanation: True. In October 2012, a Taliban gunman shot Malala on her school bus because she advocated for girls' education.

items.text: Malala wrote a book called "I Am Malala".
items.explanation: True. Her autobiography "I Am Malala" was published in 2013 and became an international bestseller.

items.text: Malala studied at Cambridge University in England.
items.explanation: Wrong. Malala studied at OXFORD University, not Cambridge. She graduated in 2020 with a PPE degree (Philosophy, Politics, Economics).

items.text: Malala lives in the United States now.
items.explanation: Wrong. After the attack, Malala moved to BIRMINGHAM, England, where she still lives with her family.

items.text: The Malala Fund helps girls around the world get an education.
items.explanation: True. Malala co-founded the Malala Fund with her father in 2013. It works in many countries to remove barriers to girls' education.

items.text: Malala speaks only Urdu — she doesn't speak English.
items.explanation: Wrong. Malala speaks Urdu, Pashto, AND fluent English. She gave her Nobel speech in English.

items.text: Before the attack, Malala wrote a blog for the BBC about life under the Taliban.
items.explanation: True. At age 11, she wrote a blog (under a pen name) for BBC Urdu about her life and the threats to girls' education.

items.text: Malala's father did NOT support her education.
items.explanation: Wrong. Her father, Ziauddin Yousafzai, was a teacher and ran a school. He strongly supported Malala's education from the beginning.
